Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Lifting Stay For The Limited Purposes Of Allowing Motion To Determine Faith Settlement; Mpa; Declaration Of Bryan G. Ceglio
Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MAURICE TROTTER's Motion For Order Lifting Stay For The Limited Purposes Of Allowing Motion To Determine Faith Settlement.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MAURICE TROTTER'S motion to lift the stay for the limited purpose of determining whether the settlement between plaintiff and Trotter is in good faith is granted. There is good cause to lift the stay because the good faith determination does not frustrate the arbitrator's jurisdiction. (See CCP 1281.4; MKJA, Inc. v. 123 Fit Franchising, LLC (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 643, 660.) The court does not recognize the application for determination of good faith settlement that Trotter filed on May 19, 2021. First, it was prematurely filed before the stay was lifted. Second, it was not served personally or by certified mail in compliance with CCP 877.6(a)(2). Trotter has leave to re-serve and file his application for good faith settlement in accordance with CCP 877.6(a)(2) or file a motion for determination of good faith settlement in compliance with CCP 877.6(a)(1).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
